theory.canonical_statement: ID 496 defines the physical safety barriers for human proximal operation. Although the Trawin Zero Point operates without traditional nuclear fuels, the high frequency electromagnetic oscillations and Casimir flux require a passive buffer. The liquid nitrogen jacket and SS304L torus provide primary shielding against non ionizing radiation and thermal leakage. Passive Shielding: The 1.185 ratio thickness of the buffer wall provides optimal damping of parasitic harmonics. Human Interface Safety: Ensures that the "Handshake" signal remains within the biological absorption limits defined in ID 478.
